bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Is the oldest of six children, including three sisters and two brothers
  • Born in Kenya and moved to the United States in September 2009
  • Upon arrival in the U.S., he only knew a few English phrases and carried one suitcase
  • Began running competitively at age 20 and called fellow 2016 U.S. Olympic teammate, Galen Rupp, his hero
  • Competed in both cross-country, and track and field at Iona College
  • Eight-time NCAA All-American.
Career Highlights
  • 2016 New York City Half Marathon, gold
  • 2011 NCAA Indoor Championships, 1st (5,000m)
  • 2011 NCAA Outdoor Championships, 1st (10,000m)
Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, 63th (Marathon - Men)
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 14th (10000m - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2018 – (Half Marathon - Men), 7th (Half Marathon Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Half Marathon - Men 2018; Half Marathon Team - Men 2018; 10000m - Men 2017; Individual Cross Country Long Distance - Men 2017; Team Cross Country Long Distance - Men 2017
  • Top finish: – 2018 (Half Marathon - Men)

bio.education: Service High School (Anchorage, Alaska) '09 University of Alaska Fiarbanks '13

bio.education: Las Lomas High School Stanford University, Communications '13
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Favorite Food: Mexican
  • Favorite Athletes: Andrew Luck and Nastia Liukin
  • Inspiration: Mom
  • Favorite Movies: Zoolander and Stepbrothers
  • Favorite TV Shows: Grey's Anatomy, Orange Is The New Black, and Revenge
  • Mariya has dual citizenship (U.S. and Russia) and she loves chocolate and tuna melts
  • She can speak three languages and is looking to learn a fourth
  • She is the daughter of Nikolay and Svetlana Koroleva, and has one brother, Ivan

  • 2011 FINA World Trophy: 5th - Team
  • 2011 Collegiate National Championships: 1st - Team, 1st - Duet
  • 2010 Collegiate National Championships: 3rd - Duet, 2nd - Trio

Quick Facts
  • Introduced to rowing during his high school freshman year
  • Has been on three consecutive National teams (2022-2024)
  • Rows in both single and double sculls
  • Hobbies include spending time with friends and playing Jenga
World Championships Experience

Quick Facts
  • Son of Joseph and Joanna Kovacs
  • Had a breakout season in 2015, capturing the USATF outdoor, world outdoor and Diamond League shot put titles, and finishing the season ranked No. 1 in the world in shot put
  • Speaks German
  • Also played football in high school
  • Enjoys playing golf during his time off in the fall
  • Is an investor in drone technology and solar companies
  • Has traveled to more than 30 countries
  • Hobbies include flying airplanes, golfing and eating good food
Olympic Experience
  • 3-time Olympian; 3-time Olympic medalist (3 silver)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, silver (Shot Put - Men)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (Shot Put - Men)
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, silver (Shot Put - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – bronze (Shot Put - Men)
  • Years of participation: Shot Put - Men 2015, 2017, 2023
  • Medals: 3 (1 gold, 1 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Gold – 2015 (Shot Put - Men)
    • Silver – 2017 (Shot Put - Men)
    • Bronze – 2023 (Shot Put - Men)

bio.education: North Carolina State University ‘17, Sport Management
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Craig and Helen Kozeniesky
  • Married to Blair Gruendl
  • Started shooting in 2009
  • Became the first Wolfpack All-American since 1975, and in 2016, and became the first shooter from North Carolina State University to compete at the Olympic Games Rio 2016
  • Opened his own shooting consultation business focused on supporting youth athletes
  • Hobbies include video games, working out, spending time with friends and competitive shooting.
Career Highlights
  • 2019 Pan American Games, 1st (10-meter air rifle), 2nd (mixed team air)
Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (10m Air Rifle - Team - Mixed), 6th (10m Air Rifle - Men)
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 21th (10m Air Rifle - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – 28th (10m Air Rifle - Team - Mixed), 39th (10m Air Rifle - Men), 57th (50m Rifle 3 Positions - Men)
  • Years of participation: 10m Air Rifle - Men 2018, 2022, 2023; 10m Air Rifle - Team - Mixed 2018, 2022, 2023; 50m Rifle 3 Positions - Men 2018, 2023; 10m Air Rifle - Team - Men 2022; 50m Rifle 3 Positions - Team - Men 2022; 50m Rifle 3 Positions - Team - Mixed 2022; 50m Rifle Prone - Men 2018, 2022; 50m Rifle Prone - Team (Mixed) - Mixed 2022; 50m Rifle Prone - Team - Men 2018
  • Medals: 1 (1 silver)
    • Silver – 2018 (50m Rifle Prone - Team - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Son of Rob and Sherry Krajicek
  • Father played college basketball at Capital University in Columbus, Ohio, and mother was a professional water skier
  • Is a distant relative of former Wimbledon champion Richard Krajicek
  • Trained at the prestigious IMG/Bollettieri Tennis Academy
  • Enjoys playing golf, basketball, fishing, hunting and reading.
Career Highlights
  • Won the ATP Tour men's singles title in the 2020 Austrian Open
  • Won the ATP Tour men's singles title in the 2019 Atlanta Open
  • Won the ATP Tour men's singles title in the 2019 Rosmalen Championships
  • Won the ATP Tour men's singles title in the 2018 Kremlin Cup
Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, silver (Doubles - Men)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 4th (Doubles - Men)
More
ATP World Tour

Quick Facts
  • Full name is John Michael Douglas Kremer
  • Parents are John and Holly Kremer
  • Brother is Jared Kremer and sisters are Jessica and Jemma Kremer
  • Wife is Gabrielle Kremer and they have a daughter named Adalyn born in 2010
  • Happiest moment in life was "when my daughter was born."
  • Hobbies are kayaking and fishing
  • Favorite book is Lone Survivor
Career Highlights
  • 2019: Parapan American Games (Silver)
  • 2016: ParaVolley Intercontinental Cup (6th place)
  • 2015: Papan American Games (Silver); Sarajevo Open (5th Place)
  • ---
  • MOST NOTABLE
  • Selected Best Receiver at 2019 Parapan American Games
  • Was the starting libero for all six matches at the 2016 Intercontinental Cup
  • Named 2015 USA Volleyball Male Sitting Most Improved Player
  • Played in 2014 Invictus Games, helping U.S. Sitting Team to Silver Medal in London
  • ---
  • DID YOU KNOW
  • Served nine years in U.S. Navy as Explosive Ordnance Disposal Technician 1st Class
  • Was injured in Afghanistan in 2010 when he stepped on a land mine, subsequently losing both legs below the knee
  • Served as captain of U.S. Team in the 2014 Invictus Games in London
  • Started playing Sitting Volleyball in the Warrior Games and landed a spot on the U.S. Men's Sitting Volleyball A2 Team
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 8th (Team - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – 6th (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2018, 2022
  • Top finish: 6th – 2022 (Team - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Wears No. 21
  • Parents are Ken and Susan Kretschman
  • Personal motto: Have fun! If you aren't having fun in what you are doing, it's not worth doing at all!
  • What non-softball sport would she like to try: bobsledding
  • On what it means to play for USA Softball: "The greatest honor anyone could ever have. It's a dream come true to wear USA across your chest and represent the greatest country in the world."
Career Highlights
  • 2018 Japan All-Star Series; Hit .333 (3-for-9)
  • 2018 Japan Cup; Hit .125 (1-for-8) with one RBI
  • 2018 USA Softball International Cup; Hit .625 (10-for-16) with two RBI
  • 2007 Pan American Games Gold Medalist; Hit .250 (2-for-8) with one run scored
  • 2007 World Cup of Softball Gold Medalist; Hit .346 (4-for-11) with two RBI and one home run
  • 2005 World Cup of Softball Silver Medalist; Hit .429 (3-for-7) with one RBI
  • 2003 Gold Medalist at Canada Cup with Elite Team; Hit .355 (11-for-31) with six RBI
Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 2-time Olympic medalist (1 gold, 1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2008, silver (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Athens 2004, gold (Team - Women)

bio.education: Forest Park Senior High School (Dumfries, Va.) ‘03 Penn State University ‘07, Advertising and Public Relations
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Ken and Debra Krieger
  • Has one brother, Kyle, whom she calls her hero
  • Began her professional soccer career in Frankfurt, Germany, where she helped Frankfurt to the 2011 German Cup title
  • Allocated to the Washington Spirit for the inaugural season of the NWSL
  • Has a tattoo the word, Liebe, which means “love” in German
  • Krieger means “warrior” in German and her teammates called her “The Warrior Princess”
  • Broke her leg during her final year at Penn State University; several plane flights during the College Cup led to blood clots in her leg that resulted in a pulmonary embolism and a series of mini heart-attacks
  • Coached by her father, Ken, from ages 7-19
  • Speaks fluent German
  • Hopes to pursue a career in sports broadcasting and continue to positively impact women’s soccer around the world after retiring from the sport
  • Never leaves the house without wearing mascara, which she calls her “war paint”
  • Interests include longboarding, practicing yoga, hiking, golfing and dancing.
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 5th (Team - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2019 – gold (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2011, 2015, 2019
  • Medals: 3 (2 gold, 1 silver)
    • Gold – 2019 (Team - Women); 2015 (Team - Women)
    • Silver – 2011 (Team - Women)
